truth.papers.A33.title: Unprecedented combination of specific tensile strength and ductility achieved in a novel duplex low-density steel
truth.papers.A33.paper_scope: Development of a novel duplex low-density steel (30Mn-13Al-1.3C-5Cr) with austenite-ferrite microstructure achieving high specific tensile strength and ductility, and investigation of the microstructural mechanisms.

prediction.papers.A33.title: Unprecedented combination of specific tensile strength and ductility achieved in a novel duplex low-density steel
prediction.papers.A33.paper_scope: Development of a Fe-Mn-Al-C-Cr austenite-ferrite duplex lightweight steel with ultralow density (6.36 g·cm⁻³) and an unprecedented specific strength-ductility synergy (SUTS×TE exceeding 8 GPa·g⁻¹·cm³·%) via annealing a cold-rolled sheet at 1000°C for 5 min.

prediction.alloys.alloy_30mn13al1c5cr.alloys_notes: Ultralow density of 6.36 g·cm⁻³ measured by buoyancy balance according to Archimedes Principle; Cr addition is intended to suppress intergranular κ-carbides.

truth.processes.proc_30mn13al1c5cr_cr.descript: Cold rolled to 3 mm thickness after homogenization at 1200 °C for 2 h, hot rolling to 6 mm, solution treatment at 1050 °C for 0.5 h and water quenching.
truth.processes.proc_30mn13al1c5cr_950_1.descr: Cold rolled sheet annealed at 950 °C for 1 min followed by water quenching.
truth.processes.proc_30mn13al1c5cr_950_5.descr: Cold rolled sheet annealed at 950 °C for 5 min followed by water quenching.
truth.processes.proc_30mn13al1c5cr_1000_1.desc: Cold rolled sheet annealed at 1000 °C for 1 min followed by water quenching.
truth.processes.proc_30mn13al1c5cr_1000_5.desc: Cold rolled sheet annealed at 1000 °C for 5 min followed by water quenching.
truth.processes.proc_30mn13al1c5cr_1050_1.desc: Cold rolled sheet annealed at 1050 °C for 1 min followed by water quenching.

prediction.processes.proc_30mn13al1c5cr_cr.descript: Vacuum induction melting under argon, casting, homogenization at 1200°C for 2 h, forging, hot rolling to 6 mm, solution treatment at 1050°C for 0.5 h, water quench, and cold rolling to 3 mm.
prediction.processes.proc_30mn13al1c5cr_cr.processe: Base thermomechanical processing prior to final annealing.
prediction.processes.proc_30mn13al1c5cr_950_1.descr: Base processing followed by annealing at 950°C for 1 min and water quenching.
prediction.processes.proc_30mn13al1c5cr_950_5.descr: Base processing followed by annealing at 950°C for 5 min and water quenching.
prediction.processes.proc_30mn13al1c5cr_1000_1.desc: Base processing followed by annealing at 1000°C for 1 min and water quenching.
prediction.processes.proc_30mn13al1c5cr_1000_5.desc: Base processing followed by annealing at 1000°C for 5 min and water quenching.
prediction.processes.proc_30mn13al1c5cr_1050_1.desc: Base processing followed by annealing at 1050°C for 1 min and water quenching.

prediction.processing_steps.sample_CR.processing_st: Rolled to 3 mm thickness from 6 mm.

truth.structures.sample_DP950_5.overall_struct: Partially recrystallized austenite with high ferrite fraction (α and δ), Cr7C3 carbides and intergranular κ-carbides.
truth.structures.sample_DP950_5.microstructure: Recrystallized austenite fraction 54.8%, ferrite fraction (XRD) 30.5%, α ferrite (EBSD) 23.8%, δ ferrite (EBSD) 12.1%; coarse Cr7C3 carbides at grain boundaries and within δ bands, intergranular κ-carbides of 2-3 μm.
partially recrystallized, containing unrecrystallized regions with shear bands
granular α ferrite and banded δ ferrite, δ bands thickest in this condition
several hundred nm to several μm
at grain boundaries and within δ ferrite bands
at grain boundaries and BCC/FCC interfaces
truth.structures.sample_DP1000_5.overall_struc: Fully recrystallized austenite matrix with granular α ferrite, banded δ ferrite, and high-density intragranular κ-carbide nanoparticles.
truth.structures.sample_DP1000_5.microstructur: Recrystallized austenite fraction 95.2%, austenite grain size 7.40±5.13 μm; ferrite fraction (XRD) 17.8%, α ferrite (EBSD) 4.1%, δ ferrite (EBSD) 9.3%; κ-carbide nanoparticle size 0.86±0.20 nm, volume fraction 1.56%.
recrystallized equiaxed grains with annealing twins
intragranular, fine dispersion within austenite grains
truth.structures.sample_DP1050_1.overall_struc: Fully recrystallized austenite with reduced ferrite fractions, negligible Cr7C3 and intergranular κ-carbides due to dissolution.
truth.structures.sample_DP1050_1.microstructur: Recrystallized austenite fraction 96.8%, austenite grain size 9.65±8.17 μm; α ferrite (EBSD) 2.7%, δ ferrite (EBSD) 4.6%.
recrystallized grains with annealing twins, larger due to grain growth

prediction.structures.sample_CR.overall_structure: Cold-rolled duplex microstructure with austenite and ferrite
prediction.structures.sample_CR.microstructure_list: granular at triple points and bandlike elongated along RD
granular 0.6–1.8 μm; banded length 12–30 μm, thickness 2–6 μm
prediction.structures.sample_DP950_1.overall_struct: Partially recrystallized austenite with high ferrite fraction
prediction.structures.sample_DP950_5.overall_struct: Partially recrystallized austenite with high ferrite fraction and intergranular carbides
prediction.structures.sample_DP950_5.microstructure: hundreds of nanometers to several micrometers
coarse particles in ferrite bands and hundreds-of-nanometers at grain boundaries
prediction.structures.sample_DP1000_5.overall_struc: Fully recrystallized austenite-ferrite duplex with intragranular κ-carbide nanoparticles
prediction.structures.sample_DP1000_5_TD.overall_st: Fully recrystallized austenite-ferrite duplex with intragranular κ-carbide nanoparticles (same microstructure as DP1000-5)
prediction.structures.sample_DP1050_1.overall_struc: Fully recrystallized austenite-ferrite duplex with coarser austenite grains

truth.interfaces.sample_DP1000_5.defect_intera: Tensile cracks nucleate at α/γ interfaces and propagate along ND. When encountering a δ ferrite band parallel to RD, the crack is deflected to propagate along RD. The crack may then blunt within the soft δ ferrite due to dislocation emission and local plastic dissipation.
truth.interfaces.sample_DP1000_5.interface_not: Strain incompatibility arises at ferrite/austenite interfaces due to strength/hardness difference; decreased fraction of ferrite and full recrystallization in DP1000-5 improve compatibility.
truth.interfaces.sample_DP1000_5.phase_evoluti: During annealing at 950 °C, austenite partially transforms to ferrite, Cr7C3, and intergranular κ-carbides. Above 950 °C, ferrite transforms back to austenite, and carbides dissolve; at 1000 °C, complete recrystallization and dissolution of detrimental carbides, while high Mn/Al/C in austenite leads to fine κ-carbide reprecipitation during water quenching.

prediction.interfaces.sample_DP1000_5.defect_intera: crack nucleation at α/γ interface and deflection by δ-ferrite bands
Cracks nucleate at α/γ interfaces, propagate along ND, are captured and deflected by δ ferrite bands parallel to RD, then blunt via dislocation emission and local plastic dissipation.
prediction.interfaces.sample_DP1000_5.interface_not: In DP950 samples, incompatible straining at ferrite/austenite interfaces due to large strength/hardness difference causes easy crack initiation; in DP1000-5, reduced ferrite fraction and fully recrystallized austenite enable more compatible deformation.
prediction.interfaces.sample_DP1000_5.phase_evoluti: With increasing annealing temperature from 950°C to 1050°C, ferrite fraction decreases, Cr7C3 and intergranular κ-carbides dissolve, austenite recrystallizes and grain growth occurs as α ferrite restraint diminishes.
prediction.interfaces.sample_DP1000_5_TD.defect_int: crack initiation at δ/γ and α/γ interfaces perpendicular to tensile direction
δ/γ interfaces perpendicular to TD are subjected to high de-cohesive stress; cracks initiate at these interfaces and quickly connect across the full thickness, leading to poor ductility.
prediction.interfaces.sample_DP1000_5_TD.interface_: When δ/γ interfaces are perpendicular to the tensile direction, high de-cohesive stress promotes interface cracking and rapid thickness-wise connection.

prediction.performance.sample_DP1000_5.comparative_: 2000 series aerospace aluminum alloys; Ti6Al4V; duplex and austenitic Fe-Mn-Al-C steels
prediction.performance.sample_DP1050_1.comparative_: 2000 series aerospace aluminum alloys; Ti6Al4V; duplex and austenitic Fe-Mn-Al-C steels

truth.characterization_methods.sample_DP1000_5: Local mechanical response of microstructural constituents
Ferrite is softer than recrystallized austenite, explaining lower YS in specimens with more ferrite.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_CR.purpo: Phase identification and ferrite fraction measurement
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_CR.key_f: Austenite (FCC) and ferrite (BCC) peaks detected in CR; β-Mn phase found at 700–900°C annealing; κ-carbides and Cr7C3 at 950–1000°C.
Identification of carbide particles in DP950-5
Cr7C3 carbides identified: coarse particles in ferrite bands and hundreds-of-nanometer particles at grain boundaries; intergranular κ-carbides 2–3 μm at BCC/FCC interfaces.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_DP1000_5: Phase mapping, grain orientation, and recrystallization fraction measurement
Phase mappings and GROD mappings show fully recrystallized austenite, granular α-ferrite, and banded δ-ferrite; Rexed austenite fraction 95.2%.
Crack path observation and interface analysis

Crack nucleation at α/γ and δ/γ interfaces observed; δ ferrite bands parallel to RD deflect ND-propagating cracks in RD specimen, while TD specimen shows cracks initiating at perpendicular δ/γ interfaces.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Compared calculated equilibrium phase fractions with measured ferrite fractions by XRD (Fig. S1). Discrepancy at lower temperatures due to kinetically arrested transformation.

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Measured ferrite fractions included for comparison in Fig. S1; calculated ferrite fraction ~33% between 991°C and 1250°C

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The specific ultimate tensile strength (SUTS) of DP1000-5 is 196.5±2.4 MPa·g^{-1}·cm^{3} and the product of SUTS and total elongation exceeds 8 GPa·g^{-1}·cm^{3}·%.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: SYS (specific yield strength) of DP1000-5 is 162.7±0.8 MPa·g^{-1}·cm^{3}.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: The previous record SUTS (169.9 MPa·g^{-1}·cm^{3}) was held by a 29.05Mn-12.01Al-1.48C-5.13Cr austenitic steel with TE 43.8% and density 6.34 g·cm^{-3}.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: β-Mn phase was detected at 700-900 °C, known to be hard and cause brittle fracture when located at grain boundaries.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: κ-carbide precipitation at 950-1000 °C consumes Mn/Al/C, leading to reduced austenite lattice parameter and XRD peak shift to higher 2θ.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Granular α ferrite grains pin austenite grain boundaries during annealing, retarding grain growth.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: In DP1000-5-TD, δ/γ interfaces are perpendicular to tensile direction, causing additional crack initiation at these interfaces and lower ductility.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: Duplex microstructural design with ferrite in two morphologies and nanosized κ-carbide precipitation enables strength-ductility-density synergy beyond existing lightweight steels.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The product of specific ultimate tensile strength (SUTS) and total elongation (TE) exceeds 8 GPa·g⁻¹·cm³·% for DP1000-5 and DP1050-1, which is higher than 2000 series Al alloys (<4), Ti6Al4V (<4), reported duplex (<6.5) and austenitic (<7.4) Fe-Mn-Al-C steels.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: The record benchmark prior to this work was 29.05Mn-12.01Al-1.48C-5.13Cr austenitic steel with SUTS of 169.9 MPa·g⁻¹·cm³, TE of 43.8%, and density of 6.34 g·cm⁻³.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Engineering stress-strain curves for all annealed specimens are presented in Fig. 3a, but no digitized coordinate data is provided in the text.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Calculated equilibrium ferrite fraction remains almost constant at about 33% between 991°C and 1250°C and increases below 854°C, whereas measured ferrite fraction peaks at 900°C due to transformation kinetics.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: XRD diffraction intensity values in the provided text table are approximate relative intensities (e.g., ~0.8 at 40° for 1050°C) and not absolute phase fractions; actual ferrite fractions were determined via supplementary methods.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: β-Mn phase was detected at 700–900°C annealing and is described as a hard phase usually formed at grain boundaries, leading to brittle fracture.
